title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Phlegm
intro: Herbal teas for Phlegm are a type of tea made from various herbs and plants that help to clear mucus and congestion in the body, reducing symptoms of coughing, sneezing, and congestion. These teas can treat phlegm ailments because they contain natural expectorants and decongestants that help to break down and expel mucus from the lungs and airways. Examples of herbal teas that can help to treat phlegm include ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling and ease congestion, and thyme tea, which contains compounds that help to loosen and clear mucus. Other herbal teas that can be effective in treating phlegm include peppermint tea, which helps to ease coughing and congestion, eucalyptus tea, which has decongestant properties to clear airways, and licorice root tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties to soothe the throat and reduce coughing.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat the phlegm ailment, particularly in respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, help to thin and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel. The bioactive constituents, such as eucalyptol and cineole, have been shown to exhibit mucolytic and bronchodilatory effects, which aid in relieving congestion and promoting easier breathing. The benefits of using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat the phlegm ailment include rapid relief from coughs and congestion, improved respiratory function, and a reduction in the risk of complications associated with respiratory infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves.
Drink the tea while it's warm, 2-3 times a day to help relieve phlegm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine: eCAM", Eucalyptus globulus teas for phlegm, based on a study reviewing traditional medicinal plants used in Ethiopia to treat respiratory tract infections, Eucalyptus globulus was among the top-cited plant species used in the treatment of respiratory disorders, specifically noted for its use in treating various respiratory issues, including phlegm.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be effective in treating phlegm ailments, but they may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications due to their active compounds like eucalyptol and cineole. To use Eucalyptus globulus teas safely, it is essential to drink them in moderation, avoid consuming them before surgery or with other herbs that may cause bleeding, and keep them out of reach of children and pets to prevent accidental ingestion.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Eucalyptus globulus tea per day
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves per cup
Steep for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds
Drink tea 2-3 times a day for 5-7 days
Monitor phlegm reduction and adjust dosage as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat phlegm ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system, thereby alleviating phlegm-related symptoms. By promoting the clearance of mucus and reducing congestion, Echinacea purpurea teas help to treat phlegm ailments such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat phlegm ailments include reduced symptoms, improved respiratory function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Echinacea flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the Echinacea flowers.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve phlegm.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can potentially cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and swelling, in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize side effects, it's essential to start with small doses, monitor your body's response, and avoid using Echinacea purpurea teas if you have a known allergy to plants in the daisy family or are taking immunosuppressive medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea per cup of boiling water.
Drink tea on an empty stomach for best results.
Continue for 5-7 days to help reduce phlegm production.
Consult a doctor before using for extended periods or with medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the root of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat phlegm-related ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber officinale teas, including gingerols and shogaols, help to break down and expel mucus from the respiratory system, providing relief from congestion and coughs. This herbal preparation helps to treat phlegm by reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of excess mucus, ultimately alleviating symptoms such as coughing and shortness of breath. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat phlegm ailments include a reduction in respiratory symptoms, improved lung function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) root.
Wash the ginger root with clean water and peel it.
Chop the ginger root into small pieces and put them in a saucepan.
Add the cup of water to the saucepan and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat, let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea and serve hot.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Zingiber officinale teas for phlegm have been found to enhance the antimicrobial potency of berberine and increase the accumulation of EtBr, suggesting its potential use in treating infections and phlegm-related issues.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be beneficial in treating the phlegm ailment, but possible side effects include nausea, stomach upset, and digestive issues due to its strong ginger flavor and potential interaction with certain medications. To minimize these side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount, also be cautious when consuming Zingiber officinale teas if you have a sensitive stomach or are prone to heartburn.
preparation_usage: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root to the water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea 2-3 times a day as needed.
Use for 7-10 days to help reduce phlegm and congestion.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat the phlegm ailment due to their antitussive and expectorant properties, which help to relieve cough and congestion. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to their therapeutic effects by reducing inflammation and suppressing the production of mucus. By reducing phlegm production and inflammation, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help to alleviate symptoms associated with the phlegm ailment, such as coughing and shortness of breath. Regular consumption of these teas may also provide additional benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to promote overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Glycyrrhiza glabra roots or 2 teaspoons of dried roots.
Cut the roots into small pieces and place them in a saucepan with 2 cups of water.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, then remove from heat.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes. Strain the liquid into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve phlegm and cough.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for phlegm were found to have a more potent antitussive effect on cough induced by citric acid compared to other studied medicinal plants.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat phlegm ailments, but excessive consumption may lead to side effects such as water retention, high blood pressure, and an increased risk of potassium deficiency due to the licorice root's potential to disrupt the body's natural potassium balance. To minimize risks, it is essential to consume Glycyrrhiza glabra teas in moderation, ideally with meals, and monitor blood pressure and potassium levels closely, especially for individuals with pre-existing conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots daily.
Consume for 7-10 days to see noticeable reduction in phlegm.
Monitor your phlegm levels and adjust dosage as needed.
Consult a healthcare professional before long-term use.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, derived from the leaves of the thyme plant, have been traditionally used to treat phlegm ailments due to their expectorant properties, which help in relieving congestion and promoting the elimination of excess mucus from the respiratory system. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as thymol and carvacrol, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion. By facilitating the removal of phlegm and reducing inflammation, Thymus vulgaris teas provide relief from coughs, colds, and respiratory infections associated with phlegm ailments, making it a natural and effective remedy for promoting respiratory health. The benefits of using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat phlegm ailments include improved respiratory function, reduced risk of complications, and a natural approach to managing symptoms without the side effects associated with conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water until it's boiling.
Add 1 tablespoon of Thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Wiadomosci lekarskie (Warsaw, Poland : 1960)", Thymus vulgaris teas for phlegm may be beneficial due to the spasmolytic and expectorant properties of thymol and saponines in Thymus vulgaris, which can help alleviate cough and shorten the duration of upper respiratory tract infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat phlegm ailments, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, gastrointestinal upset, and interactions with certain medications. Precautions should be taken when using Thymus vulgaris teas, such as monitoring blood pressure, avoiding excessive consumption, and being cautious with individuals with sensitive stomachs or taking antacids.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea, 2-3 times a day
Take 1 tablespoon of thyme extract, 2-3 times a day
Use 2-3 teaspoons of dried thyme leaves, 2-3 times a day
Use 10-15 drops of thyme essential oil, 2-3 times a day
Combine with other herbs, like eucalyptus, for better results

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, a herbal preparation derived from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to treat phlegm ailments due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and ursolic acid, help to break down and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of phlegm. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat phlegm include improved respiratory function, reduced congestion, and a decrease in the production of mucus. Regular consumption of this herbal preparation may also help to prevent the recurrence of phlegm ailments, making it a valuable natural remedy for those suffering from respiratory issues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat to a simmer.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves from the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve phlegm.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can help alleviate phlegm ailments, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, diarrhea, or stomach upset due to its potential to interact with certain medications and exacerbate sensitive stomach conditions. Precautions should be taken to avoid using Rosmarinus officinalis teas in high doses or for extended periods, as this may lead to dizziness, headaches, or allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with allergies or sensitivities to herbs in the Lamiaceae family.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Rosmarinus officinalis tea daily.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herbs per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors to release.
Use 2-3 times a day, morning, noon, and evening, as needed.
Monitor phlegm levels and adjust dosage or frequency accordingly.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint teas, have been traditionally used to treat phlegm-related ailments due to their natural expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Drink 1 cup of the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day, with or without honey to taste.
Consult a doctor or a healthcare professional before using Ginkgo biloba for medical purposes, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to help alleviate phlegm symptoms, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those who are sensitive to ginkgo's unique compounds. Use 1-2 tablespoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves per cup.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas, a traditional herbal remedy, have been used for centuries to treat phlegm-related ailments, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, help to treat phlegm by loosening and clearing mucus from the respiratory tract. The bioactive constituents of Cinnamomum verum, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, possess antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties that aid in reducing inflammation and relieving cough and congestion. The benefits of this herbal preparation include rapid relief from phlegm-related symptoms, improved respiratory function, and a reduced risk of secondary infections, making it a popular choice for treating respiratory ailments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of Cinnamomum verum (Ceylon Cinnamon) bark
Bring 1 cup of water to boil in a saucepan
Add 2 tablespoons of Cinnamomum verum to the boiling water
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes
Strain and drink warm to help with phlegm
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided scientific study, Cinnamomum verum teas for phlegm is not mentioned directly, however, Cinnamomi cortex, one of the components of Sairei-To, is mentioned as part of the formula, but no specific information is provided about its use in teas for phlegm.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can help to treat phlegm ailment due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, but it may also cause side effects such as digestive issues, diarrhea, and stomach cramps in some individuals. To use Cinnamomum verum teas safely, it is recommended to consume it in moderation and with warm water, avoid taking it with other medications, and be cautious of potential allergic reactions such as skin rashes and itching.

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas, also known as fennel tea, have been traditionally used to treat phlegm-related ailments due to its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract. The herbal preparation's ability to ease congestion and coughs is attributed to the presence of bioactive constituents such as anethole, fenchone, and trans-anethole, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. By promoting the expulsion of mucus and reducing inflammation, fennel tea helps to alleviate symptoms of phlegm-related ailments, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for respiratory issues. The benefits of using fennel tea to treat phlegm-related ailments include its natural and non-invasive approach, which can be especially helpful for individuals who are sensitive to pharmaceutical medications or prefer a holistic approach to healthcare.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 1 minute.
Add the dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the pot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer and discard the seeds.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve phlegm and congestion.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Fortschritte der Medizin, Foeniculum vulgare teas for phlegm increased the mucociliary transport rate by a certain extent, ranking third in effectiveness after Folia farfarae (coltsfoot) and fructus foeniculi (fennel) in a study on isolated ciliated epithelium of the frog oesophagus.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be used to treat the phlegm ailment, but possible side effects include digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, due to the anethole content in the tea, as well as allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions include taking the teas in moderation, as excessive consumption may exacerbate the symptoms, and avoiding consumption if you have a history of stomach ulcers or are pregnant or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Piper nigrum teas, also known as black pepper tea, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various respiratory issues, including the phlegm ailment. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as piperine, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce mucus production and alleviate congestion. By reducing inflammation and breaking down mucus, Piper nigrum teas help to treat the phlegm ailment by making it easier to expel excess mucus from the body, providing relief from coughing and congestion. The benefits of using Piper nigrum teas to treat the phlegm ailment include improved respiratory function, reduced risk of secondary infections, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Piper nigrum and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried Piper nigrum to a cup and pour in the boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to combine.
Strain the mixture into another cup to remove the Piper nigrum.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve phlegm.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Piper nigrum teas can be used to treat phlegm ailments, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it's essential to brew the tea in moderation, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and monitor your body's response to the herbal preparation, gradually reducing the dosage if symptoms persist or worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, have been traditionally used to treat the phlegm ailment due to their expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by loosening and thinning mucus, making it easier to expel, and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract. The bioactive constituents of lemongrass tea, including citral and geraniol, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, as they exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities that help to combat the underlying causes of phlegm. The benefits of using lemongrass tea to treat phlegm include its natural, non-addictive, and cost-effective approach, making it an attractive alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 1 tablespoon of honey.
Wash the leaves with water, then dry them with a clean cloth.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Cymbopogon citratus leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add the honey. Stir well to dissolve the honey.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve phlegm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of food biochemistry, Cymbopogon citratus teas combined with green tea may potentially enhance the health promoting potential and antioxidant properties, but no direct information is provided about using Cymbopogon citratus teas specifically for phlegm.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be used to treat the phlegm ailment, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions to take when using Cymbopogon citratus teas to treat phlegm include starting with small amounts, being cautious when combining with other herbal remedies, and avoiding use if pregnant or breastfeeding due to potential hormonal effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas have been traditionally used to treat phlegm-related ailments, and their properties of being anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant help to alleviate symptoms. The herbal preparation works by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract, relieving congestion, and promoting the expulsion of excess phlegm. The bioactive constituents of Cinnamomum zeylanicum, including cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, exhibit their therapeutic effects by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and modulating the body's immune response. The benefits of using Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas to treat phlegm-related ailments include reduced coughing, improved breathing, and faster recovery times.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of dried Cinnamomum zeylanicum powder to a tea infuser or a small strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the powder and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the powder.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day to help relieve phlegm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas for phlegm are not directly mentioned in the provided study as the main focus is on the herbal medicine Sairei-To and its components, but Cinnamomum zeylanicum, or Cinnamon, is mentioned as one of the components of the Sairei-To formula.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas can be used to treat the phlegm ailment, but its long-term consumption may lead to side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To safely use Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas, it is essential to start with small amounts, gradually increase the dosage as needed, and avoid consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to potential hormonal imbalances and digestive issues in newborns.

preparations.herb_desc: Hydrastis canadensis teas, also known as goldenseal, have been traditionally used to treat phlegm-related ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents, including berberine and hydrastine, help to break down and expel mucus, reducing congestion and coughs. By acting as an expectorant and mucolytic agent, goldenseal teas help to relieve symptoms of phlegm, such as difficulty breathing and chest congestion. The benefits of using goldenseal teas to treat phlegm include rapid relief from symptoms, reduced risk of secondary infections, and a non-invasive, natural approach to managing respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Hydrastis canadensis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the root.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed for phlegm relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hydrastis canadensis teas can be used to treat phlegm ailments, but its use may lead to side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high berberine content. To minimize potential side effects, take precautions by drinking the tea in moderation, avoiding it if you have kidney or liver problems, and monitoring your body's reaction to its use.

preparation_study: According to Harefuah, Eucalyptus globulus teas for phlegm have anti-inflammatory, anti-bacterial, and anti-viral activities, which may help in the treatment of respiratory ailments.
intro_study: According to "Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y)", teas for phlegm, such as Traditional Medicinals' Throat Coat, have been shown to significantly reduce the intensity of throat pain when swallowing and provide rapid relief of sore throat pain in patients with pharyngitis.
